back_translation: The LORD said to Moses, 'One more plague I will bring upon Pharaoh and upon Egypt; after that he will let you go from here; when he lets you go completely, he will surely drive you out from here.'
'Speak now in the ears of the people, that each man ask of his neighbor and each woman of her neighbor, articles of silver and articles of gold.'
The LORD gave the people favor in the eyes of the Egyptians; moreover the man Moses was very great in the land of Egypt, in the eyes of Pharaoh's servants and in the eyes of the people.
Moses said, 'Thus says the LORD: About midnight I will go out into the midst of Egypt.'
'And every firstborn in the land of Egypt shall die, from the firstborn of Pharaoh who sits on his throne to the firstborn of the maidservant behind the mill, and every firstborn of cattle.'
'And there shall be a great cry throughout all the land of Egypt, such as has not been before and shall not be again.'
'But against any of the sons of Israel, not a dog shall move its tongue, against man or beast — so that you may know that the LORD makes a distinction between Egypt and Israel.'
'All these servants of yours shall come down to me and bow down to me, saying, Go out, you and all the people who follow you. After that I will go out.' He went out from Pharaoh in burning anger.
The LORD said to Moses, 'Pharaoh will not listen to you, so that my wonders may be multiplied in the land of Egypt.'
Moses and Aaron did all these wonders before Pharaoh; but the LORD hardened Pharaoh's heart, and he did not let the sons of Israel go out of his land.
